title: grants — artisan commands
description: Artisan commands this module ships. Registered by `GrantServiceProvider::registerCommands()`. Ops audience for support tooling + compliance evidence generation; dev audience for local seeding.

commands.description: Prints grants matching filters as a table. `--active-only` hides revoked + expired. Useful during support tickets when the tenant-facing UI doesn't surface enough context.

Prints the full row + related resolver metadata: overlay-resolution trace ('this deny wins over these three allows'), the RBAC comparison ('user held X via role Y before this grant'), audit-log link, activity-log links. Debug tool for 'why does Bob NOT see invoice #123'.

Issue a grant from the CLI. Delegates to GrantIssuer::issue() so the same guards fire. `--permissions=` accepts a comma-separated list. Requires the caller to have the tenant admin PAT via `--tenant=`.

Revoke a grant. Requires a reason of 20+ characters. Delegates to GrantRevoker::revoke() so audit + notification cascade fires. Used mostly for incident-response revocations that can't route through the tenant UI.

Bulk-issue grants from a local CSV. CSV columns: `subject_id, resource_type, resource_id, permissions, decision, expires_at, reason`. `--dry-run` validates every row without inserting. Actor-attribution required — sets granted_by on every produced grant.

Enumerates every grant on a specific resource. Sorted by decision (denies first) then created_at desc. `--include-terminal` includes revoked/expired grants. Answers 'who has access to this thing right now' + 'who has EVER had access'.
Enumerates every grant this principal holds. Cross-resource view. Feeds ISO 27001 A.9.2.5 access reviews — 'what does this user have access to via grants (outside their RBAC roles)'.
Dispatches PurgeExpiredGrantsJob. Called hourly by the scheduler + on-demand during backfills.
Sweeps grants whose resource no longer exists. Dry-run first (weekly scheduled entry) — an unusually high orphan count indicates cascade-delete bugs elsewhere in the platform.
Prints feature-flag ages + rollout state. Fires the retirement-policy alert for flags past 90d at 100% GA (per `feature-flags.json` retirement_policy).
